title: Kemal Özkırşehirli
description: Academic homepage for Kemal Özkırşehirli: AI/ML for science, chemical physics, mathematical biology, order-agnostic 3D mesh generation, computer-aided drug discovery, protein and genome modeling, molecular simulation, statistical mechanics, and auditable scientific software.

author.name: Kemal Özkırşehirli
author.role: B.Sc. candidate at MIT · GPA 5.0/5.0 · Computer Science and Engineering: AI/ML · Physics: Chemical Physics · Mathematics: Mathematical Biology · Minors in Philosophy and Writing
author.bio: I am an international student from İstanbul, Türkiye, and a B.Sc. candidate at MIT with a 5.0/5.0 GPA, pursuing a rare triple major in Computer Science and Engineering: AI/ML, Physics: Chemical Physics, and Mathematics: Mathematical Biology, with minors in Philosophy and Writing. I am on track to graduate one year early, completing all requirements for three majors and two minors in three total years of undergraduate education. I use AI/ML frameworks, generative and diffusion models, free-energy methods, atomistic molecular dynamics, Monte Carlo, density-functional theory, inference, and robust optimization to convert complex scientific systems into parsable, computable representations and extract governing rules for scientific decision-making.
highlights: Current and extended work spans MeshAnyOrder order-agnostic 3D mesh generation, TBXT/brachyury small-molecule discovery, PRISM protein-model red-teaming, ChromoGen-Engine V2, A*STAR V2M variant-to-mechanism evidence graphs, VeriQSM scientific workflow verification, EVEdesign, antibody RL, Kadanoff-GNN-RG, Kupcinet–Getz reaction–diffusion modeling, DFT → kMC, and Pedal AI chemistry-agent infrastructure.

Research and engineering across the Özkırşehirli Group, MIT Chemistry, MIT CSAIL, MIT Physics, A*STAR EDDC, PRISM/W2D2/Siemens, Harvard Medical School, Columbia, AI@MIT/AIM Labs, Weizmann Institute, Pedal AI, Sepal AI/Mercor, and the Buchwald Lab.

Medalist at IChO and IMChO; TÜBİTAK National Chemistry Olympiad silver medalist; Columbia Science Research Fellow; World Science Scholar; AIME qualifier; AlgoTrade Hackathon 2026 selectee; and recipient of Waterloo CHEM13, Galois, and Sir Isaac Newton distinctions.

selectedSections.title: Molecular Simulation, CADD, and Chemical Physics

items.title: TBXT
items.description: 11-person chordoma/TBXT hit-identification project targeting PDB 6F59 site F; 2,274 prior-art compounds, 737 analogs, 30,000 BRICS recombinations, a 570-compound novelty-filtered pool, and a docking/QSAR/GNINA/Boltz-2/MMGBSA-FEP/selectivity funnel

items.title: VeriQSM + QSMBench
items.description: verification-first scientific-agent framework for quantum chemistry and statistical mechanics: typed workflow IR, static and physics verifiers, bounded repair/refusal policies, provenance ledgers, PySCF-compatible calculations, trajectory/stat-mech analyses, and explicit separation of nominal completion from scientifically verified success

items.title: Kadanoff-GNN-RG
items.description: hierarchical-lattice renormalization-group framework for random-bond spin-s Ising models; direct continuum RG operators, finite-spin distribution comparison, symmetry-aware features, Wasserstein/MMD-style distances, and uncertainty-aware phase-topology analysis

items.title: Buchwald Lab
items.description: CuH-catalyst development for asymmetric methylation and alkylation of vinyl boronate esters, with 1H-NMR, chiral-HPLC, substrate-scope expansion, and multi-gram scale-up

items.title: Pedal AI
items.description: Python/LangGraph and FastAPI infrastructure for chemistry agents, molecular-prediction engines, inspectable retrosynthesis trees, provenance, and decision-ready synthesis workflows

items.title: Kupcinet–Getz Reaction–Diffusion AI
items.description: reaction–diffusion scientific-computing framework for nonlinear oscillators, traveling waves, and synthetic morphogenesis; deterministic ODE solvers, chemical-Langevin dynamics, Gillespie SSA, spatial RDME, convergence audits, calibration, and abstention

items.title: Sepal AI / Mercor
items.description: Graduate-level chemistry LLM data, retrosynthesis and reaction-mechanism tasks, chemical-validity rubrics, prompt analysis, and failure-mode annotation

items.title: DFT → kMC
items.description: mechanism-aware decision-stability pipeline linking Gaussian/ORCA thermochemistry to Eyring rates, ODE/CME/Gillespie kinetics, solvent/pathway/product ranking, uncertainty checks, and provenance-rich scientific reports

selectedSections.title: Biomolecular AI, Protein, and Genome Modeling

items.title: MeshAnyOrder
items.description: order-agnostic autoregressive transformer for point-cloud-conditioned 3D mesh generation with quantized face tokens, arbitrary traversal seeds, 3D RoPE, triangle/quad tokenization, topology-aware constraints, frontier-parallel decoding, and publication-grade ablations

items.title: PRISM
items.description: Adversarial evaluation of protein foundation models across biological plausibility, sequence-structure-function consistency, developability, uncertainty calibration, claimed mechanisms, and failure taxonomies

items.title: ChromoGen V2
items.description: conditioned 3D genome generation and evaluation pipeline for single cells; sequence windows, perturbation labels, regulatory readouts, coordinate ensembles, contact/P(s)/polymer realism, condition separation, calibration, leakage checks, and attribution diagnostics

items.title: V2M
items.description: provenance-first variant-to-mechanism evidence graph for IBD/autoimmune target discovery, connecting disease, locus, QTL/colocalization, effector gene, cell context, mechanism, and target-control tiers before foundation-model extensions

items.title: EVEdesign
items.description: Open-source, method-independent protein design across a 21-person, 18-institution, 8-country collaboration, with uncertainty-aware multi-objective candidate selection and lab-in-the-loop workflows

items.title: Antibody RL
items.description: risk-constrained, structure-guided policy-optimization scaffold for antibody sequence research; ESM-2-compatible antigen embeddings, structure-informed cross-attention, supervised warm starts, selective PPO, uncertainty penalties, and evidence-lineage controls

items.title: Stack
items.description: Python, C/C++, CUDA, PyTorch, Transformers, GNNs, HPC/GPU programming, Linux, Bash, Git, SLURM/PBS/qsub, RDKit, OpenMM/OpenFE, PySCF, LangGraph, FastAPI, and LaTeX

items.title: Chem/Physics/Simulation
items.description: 8.592[J] Statistical Physics in Biology; 2.094[J] Atomistic Modeling and Simulation of Materials and Structures; 8.05 Quantum Physics II; 5.601 Thermodynamics I; 5.351 Fundamentals of Spectroscopy; 5.43 Advanced Organic Chemistry

items.title: CS/AI/Systems
items.description: 6.7960 Deep Learning; 6.5060 Algorithm Engineering; 6.4610 Natural Language Processing; 6.3930 AI and Decision Making in Medicine; 6.1220[J] Advanced Algorithm Design-Analysis; 18.404[J] Theory of Computation; 18.615 Stochastic Processes; 6.5240 Sublinear Time Algorithms; 6.1910 Computer Structures; 6.1903 C and Assembly; 6.100B Computational Thinking; 6.1200[J] Discrete Mathematics; 18.600 Probability; 18.06 Linear Algebra

items.title: Humanitas Core
items.description: 24.245 Theory of Models; 21W.747 Rhetoric; 21W.745 Advanced Essay Workshop; 21W.762 Poetry Workshop; 24.251 Philosophy of Language; 24.05 Philosophy of Religion; 17.01[J]/24.04[J] Justice; 24.121 Metaphysics; 21W.738[J] Memoir

home.researchStatement: My work and the Özkırşehirli Group aim to make difficult scientific systems parsable, computable, verifiable, and decision-ready. (1) We develop MeshAnyOrder, an order-agnostic autoregressive transformer for topology-aware 3D mesh generation from point clouds. (2) We build multi-stage CADD pipelines for TBXT/brachyury, integrating docking, QSAR, CNN rescoring, co-folding, free-energy scaffolding, selectivity, and sourceability. (3) Scientific-software directions include VeriQSM + QSMBench for artifact-grounded verification of scientific false success, Kadanoff-GNN-RG for learned coarse-graining and phase analysis, Kupcinet–Getz Reaction–Diffusion AI for solver-faithful benchmarking, DFT → kMC for auditable multiscale kinetics, and a structure-informed antibody-RL scaffold. (4) I develop and evaluate generative, foundation, and agentic systems for protein design, chromatin organization, autoimmune target discovery, molecular simulation, and robust scientific decision-making.

group.description: A student-led research organization founded and led by Kemal Özkırşehirli, presenting its current research directions and software.
